reason:
Both correctly diagnose this as an existential platform risk and prescribe fast-detect telemetry/synthetic monitoring, remote config for quick patching, resilient selector strategies, and acquisition diversification. The key differentiator is that Answer A explicitly calls out converting extension users into direct users (accounts/emails) as a strategic mitigation, which is one of the core points the question calls for — Answer B omits this entirely, focusing instead on policy/ToS risk (a reasonable addition but not part of the core ask) and channel diversification without addressing direct-relationship capture. Answer A also includes a useful heuristic ('assume breakage 2-4x/year, design for hours not weeks') that operationalizes the 'don't deepen dependence' point. Answer B's leading 'Sketch' section is a bit of unpolished scratch-work presented to the reader, adding minor clutter without value. Overall A is more complete on the points that matter without being padded.</parameter>
</invoke>
